design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate destructible, non-linear levels; eliminate aliens and mutated enemies; collect weapons and power-ups; interact with environment for health/utility or narrative flavor
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammo, health) balanced against environmental interactivity and multiple tactical approaches to enemy encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Powerful, wisecracking action hero (Duke Nukem) fighting alien invasion on Earth with over-the-top Hollywood action sensibility and risqué humor
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Highly interactive, destructible environments with non-linear level design encouraging exploration; unapologetic pop-culture satire and lampooning of action-hero tropes; controversial risqué humor and adult content as core identity rather than optional flavor

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; progression saved between completed levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple selectable difficulty modes available; non-linear level design allows players to choose tactical approaches and route through environments

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon unlocks and temporary power-ups (steroids, night vision, jet pack, aqua-lung, protective boots, HoloDuke device); health restoration via medkits and environmental props
progression.unlock_mechanism: Weapons and items discovered/looted during level progression; arsenal includes Mighty Foot, pistol, shotgun, chain gun, RPG launcher, pipe bombs, freezethrower, shrink-ray, laser mines, Devastator; Atomic Edition adds Expander; 20th Anniversary adds Incinerator
replayability_hooks: Non-linear level design with multiple routes and hidden caches encouraging exploration
Destructible environment secrets and interactive props
Multiple difficulty modes (evidenced by 'Piece of Cake' speedrun category)
Environmental puzzle-solving via interactivity (light switches, water fountains)
Speedrunning community with 400+ runs tracked across episodes and difficulty variants

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through interactive props and destructible world details
Character voice lines and quips from Duke (voiced by Jon St. John) triggered by interactions
Setting-based narrative (alien invasion on Earth, Las Vegas/Hollywood locations implied)
Pop-culture satire and Hollywood action-movie lampooning embedded in level design and dialogue
Alien and mutated human enemy design conveying invasion narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Significant alien and mutated creatures serve as culminating challenges; boss encounters leverage level-specific environmental hazards and player weapon selection
notable_bosses: Environment-integrated boss encounters leveraging destructible/interactive level geometry

faction_indicators: LAPD transformed into 'Pig Cops' (mutated police with LARD emblazoned uniforms)
Alien invaders as unified threat faction
Mutated humans vs. pure humans as implicit social divide
danger_signals: Mutation of human law enforcement into hostile Pig Cops
Transformation of civilian spaces into combat zones
environmental_storytelling: Las Vegas and Hollywood locations as setting choices reflecting satire of American pop culture
Destructible civilian infrastructure conveying invasion impact
Interactive props (strip clubs, casinos, police stations) grounding narrative in satirized Americana
Non-linear sewer/back-door accessibility suggesting desperation and resistance navigation
